violations.text: Yogurt parfaits and deli sandwiches packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Guidance documents provided during the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC provided a new container of sanitizer and was tested at 100 parts per million (ppm). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2026

Various deli sandwiches were observed held out of temperature control and were observed at temperatures ranging from 67-70 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, sandwiches were placed on display at opening (7:30am) and are disposed of at the end of the end per SOP. Food items were evaluated and voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2026

Various bakery items and yogurt parfaits packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Food items were relocated out of consumer self-service areas until labels can be generated for food items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2026
